capabilities.description: Search for a Wikipedia article by topic or keyword.
examples: look up quantum physics on Wikipedia
find a Wikipedia article about climate change
search wiki for the Roman Empire
what does Wikipedia say about machine learning
disambiguationHints: Use this when the user wants to search for a topic on Wikipedia.
For general knowledge questions, this is a good default.
If the user names a specific article title, prefer open_article instead.
This opens the search results page, not a specific article.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search term or topic to look up on Wikipedia
parameters.prompt: What would you like to search for on Wikipedia?

capabilities.description: Open a specific Wikipedia article by its title.
examples: open the Wikipedia article about Python programming
read the article on World War II
show me the Wikipedia page for Tokyo
open the German article about Berlin
disambiguationHints: Use this when the user names a specific article they want to read.
If the user is exploring a topic broadly, prefer search_wikipedia instead.
The language parameter lets you open articles in other language Wikipedias.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Exact or approximate Wikipedia article title
parameters.prompt: Which article would you like to open?

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Wikipedia language code (defaults to user's preferred language)

capabilities.description: Open a random Wikipedia article for exploration and discovery.
examples: show me a random Wikipedia article
surprise me with a Wikipedia article
I'm bored, show me something interesting
disambiguationHints: Use this when the user wants to discover something new or be surprised.
Good for 'I'm bored' or 'show me something interesting' requests.
